races.name: Troll
races.bodyPlan: Quasi-humanoid: two arms, two legs, full upright stance, with torso depth, limb girth, and hand/foot size far above human baselines. Five toes; no extra limbs. Proportions are not “a scaled-up human“—shorter neck, wider rib cage, and a lower center of mass than a human of similar height.
races.silhouette: A low, wide trapezoid: massive shoulders and back tapering into thick pillar legs, with a small head sitting deep between the traps—“moving boulder” before any facial detail.
races.build: Adult height spans a wide band by region; proportions sit around 4.5–5.5 heads tall (stocky) with a short, thick neck, deep chest, and column legs. Idle posture is a slight forward lean from the hips, bracing weight through the full foot, not a sickly stoop. Adolescents are already broad but not full height, with limbs lengthening before the chest finishes thickening. Children are short and wide, with a relatively larger head, rounder belly, and short shins.
races.surface: Thick, hide-like skin: cool blue-grey to green-grey, mottled with lighter patches and deep crease shadows. Creases at jaw, elbow, and clavicle can carry lichen- or crack-like markings as species pattern (not paint). Coarse vellus on forearms and calves; knuckles and elbows often warmer grey-brown. Fingernails are thick, human in count and placement.
races.head: Skull is broad and low, with a heavy brow over small, deep-set eyes (two bright points read at 32px). Nose is wide, nostrils large. Ears are small, tight to the head or mildly pointed in some lineages—never long elf ears. The mandible is heavy; canines can be prominent, and some adults show uneven, worn tusks as keratin at the tooth line, not long sabre fangs. No true horn cores: any “horn” read should be a keratin nub or knotted scar mass, not a clean spiral.
races.anatomy: Mass is carried in the upper back, chest, and thighs; the waist is shallow at rest. Hands have thick, shorter fingers and a strong crushing grip. Feet are broad and splayed. The rib cage is laterally very wide; turns are slow. Differences from smaller humanoid races are mass, bone thickness, and weight distribution—not a new body plan.
races.variation: Sex: adults share the same overall silhouette; on average, one pattern leans to heavier jaw and deltoids, another to a slightly wider pelvis, with full overlap. Age: children read as short and broad with a big head; teens lengthen first then thicken; elders show more fissuring, jowl sag, and optional belly mass while the shoulder line stays wide. Regional (art-direction sublabels, not culture names): highland trolls can read paler with more “lichen” speckle; coastal trolls lean greener-grey with salt-weathering at the brows; inland wold trolls can pick up warmer earth undertones.
races.senses: Eyes favour twilight: pupils can look large in dim light. Hearing leans to low rumbles, footfalls, and shifting stone. Nostrils are wide for damp earth, rot, and weather—not a separate animal snout. No heat pits or echolocation.
races.lifespan: Childhood and adolescence are slow compared to human norms. Prime endures a long midlife; ageing first shows as skin fissuring, loss of mottled contrast, hollows around the eyes, and a stiffer, slower gait. Elders can remain very broad; frailty is a late look.
races.spriteNote: At 32px: prioritize torso width (often 3–4 px) and a small head (about 2×2 px) sitting low, two eye highlights or a single lit band, one heavy brow stroke, and thick legs with no fine ankle. Tusks, if used, are a 1px jaw line, not individual teeth. Crease-lichen = one midtone side pixel or skip for clarity.
races.pageBody:
Visual mood: still, geologic, of the land—Nordic troll-kin, not a small goblin or cartoon bridge-gremlin. The classic Seven Giants in Matlu are singular, territory-scale beings; this entry describes troll-kin as a people-scale anatomy that can stand near smaller folk without duplicating a named giant’s “mountain in a body” look. In settlement data the legacy id `troll` maps to the canonical Giants People (see `docs/peoples-and-races.md`); do not use clothes, tools, or architecture here.

Art direction references (not 1:1 copies): Scandinavian folklore trolls, hill- and stone-giant silhouettes, weathered bedrock and coastal spray-stone. Keep palette cool and mottled; avoid neon greens unless corruption is the subject of a different brief.

races.name: Vindfarare
races.bodyPlan: Quasi-humanoid: two arms, two legs, and an upright stance matching a human baseline, but with elongated segments, slightly long feet (tarsal emphasis, not hooves), and optional low patagial skin ribbons along the flanks that never form a true wing. Enough to read as “wind-borne” without avian muzzle or beak, and not fey or draconic.
races.silhouette: Tall, narrow vertical wedge: a small high head, wide shoulders, long legs, and a single back-swept crest or plume on the occiput — the brace that reads as “cliff-wind” at sprite scale before any face detail does.
races.build: Adults are tall and lean (about 7.0–7.5 heads tall) with a low body mass: wire, not brawler. Slightly forward-rolled but level shoulders, long neck, weight carried on the forefoot as if the ground is a temporary stop. Adolescents are awkwardly long in limb before the chest fills. Elders keep posture upright; the plume/crest thins, hands and face show more skin desiccation, shanks can read a touch thinner in sprite as “stick” legs.
races.surface: Palette leans cool: pale storm-grey, desaturated blue, sanded brown, and gull-off-white, with a wind-burn ruddiness on cheekbones, knuckles, and the bridge of the nose. Short, dense vellus on forearms, calves, and flanks, tufting into a longer plume/crest at the back of the skull and upper nape. A simple dorsal fade (slate on grey) is common; it is a species pattern, not clothing or body paint. No full pelts.
races.head: Eyes are large, a bit more lateral than a human’s, with room for a thin highlighted band under the cornea to suggest a nictitating sheen against bright sky and dust. Ears are medium, mildly pointed, with a forward ridge that flows into the crest. Nose is narrow, nostrils wide and mobile. Jaw tapers; teeth are within human class — do not add predator fangs. No horns. The crest is keratinous-feather, not bony prongs.
races.anatomy: Beyond baseline human: (1) low patagial skin lines on the flanks, relaxed when idle; (2) very low subcutaneous fat, so tendons and muscle bands read in large art, but collapse to two or three values at 32px; (3) chest slightly wide for O₂ at altitude; (4) long toes and a useful hallux grip for ledges. No true powered flight. These traits align to high exposure (steppe, cliff) without redefining the silhouette to a new body plan.
races.variation: Sex: adults share the same build envelope; on average, one pattern runs broader shoulders and a slightly fuller crest, another runs a longer leg line, but any adult can show any mix. Age: children have large heads, short legs, and no adult crest (only a downy tuft). Teens stretch in the limbs first; the crest plumes in late adolescence. Regional: eyrie lineages can read a finger stockier and paler; open-plains lineages can read longer-legged and tanner. These are soft gradients for sprite variants, not sub-race splits.
races.senses: Vision prioritises distance, motion, and high-glare open sky. Night vision is not a specialty. Hearing favours low-frequency wind and distant wing-thrum; the pinna shape still supports lateral localisation. Smell is broadly human. Wide nostrils serve dust and rapid breathing at sustained exertion, not super tracking.
races.lifespan: Physical maturation about 16–18 years, prime from roughly 20–45, then clear ageing with crest thinning, weathering of hands/face, and a slight “wind-posture” forward lean that is muscular habit, not kyphosis. No extraordinary lifespan; the elderly are still mobile in favourable climates, but the sprite can communicate age with a smaller plume and a narrower stance.
races.spriteNote: At 32px, carry identity with a two-pixel-tall head, one-bright eye highlight, one strong crest or plume stroke, a bunched shoulder line, and long legs (three- to four-pixel shanks). Flank patagial can be a single mid-tone line along the body silhouette; if the palette is tight, drop it and let crest + long legs do the work. No micro-face detail: silhouette first.
races.pageBody:
Mood: bracing, spare, high-sun and high-wind. Visual anchors: sighthound and alpine climber, not angel or gryphon — no feathered muzzle, no second pair of limbs. Fictional race for the Matlu multiworld. Settlement data now samples Viddfolk and Bergfolk mixes for the old windfarer-eyrie culture; this page is the dedicated visual spec tied to the legacy vindfarare id for sprites and the codex.

Clothes, kit, and architecture are culture. Do not use this page to lock costume — only bare anatomy and default surface variation.
